markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define how model edits are proposed, displayed, accepted, rejected, and logged. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: AiEditProposalReviewFlow leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: AiEditProposalReviewFlow is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 dbecce27; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: EXACTLY-ONCE PROMOTION -- DB-state-machine-guaranteed. promote_ai_edit_proposal flips the row via mark_ai_edit_proposal_promoted: 'UPDATE ... WHERE proposal_id=$1 AND review_state=''approved''' (knowledge_crdt.rs:1083-1105). Even if two concurrent promotions both pass the read-time review_state=='approved' check (ai_edit_proposal.rs:397), only one conditional UPDATE matches a row; the loser gets None and is mapped to AlreadyPromoted (ai_edit_proposal.rs:472-482). Plus migration 0154 chk_..._promotion (0154:70-78) enforces promotion_requested+accepted exist IFF review_state='promoted', ALWAYS as a pair -- a half-promoted row is structurally impossible. This is not scheduling luck.

Model self-approval blocked twice: runtime reviewer-kind gate (ai_edit_proposal.rs:245-252) AND DB reviewer regex CHECK '^(operator|validator):' (0154:64-68). decided fields travel together (chk_..._decision, 0154:55-63). actor_id regex restricts proposers to '^(local_model|cloud_model):' (0154:31).

APPROVED-vs-APPLIED INTEGRITY GAP (medium): promotion only flips the proposal row to 'promoted' and stamps event ids; per the header the approved diff is then 'eligible to be applied as a normal CRDT update by the model actor (push path)'. NOTHING enforces that the subsequently pushed update equals the approved diff (no check that the pushed update's content hashes to the approved diff_sha256). A model could get diff A approved then push diff B as a draft update citing the proposal. Lands in the draft log (not authority), so severity is bounded, but the approval is not cryptographically bound to the application.

LEASE existence-only (medium): record_ai_edit_proposal requires lease_id.is_some() for model actors (ai_edit_proposal.rs:112-116) and the FK (0154:35) guarantees the lease row exists, but neither calls guard_lease_for_write, so an EXPIRED/RELEASED/FOREIGN/wrong-SCOPE existing lease id passes. Same weakness as MT-068/MT-079.

source_span_citations: non-empty array CHECK (0154:26-29) + non-empty-string runtime check (knowledge_crdt.rs:959-968). Same soft-ref class as MT-068 -- citations are arbitrary strings, never validated against knowledge_spans, but AI edits do not promote into the promoted_facts authority table, so the dangling-evidence-becomes-authority concern (MT-069) does not apply to this path.
evasion_probes: Hammered two concurrent promotions of one approved proposal: only one mark_ai_edit_proposal_promoted UPDATE succeeds (conditional on review_state='approved'); the other -> AlreadyPromoted. No double promotion, no second event pair commits (idempotency keys ...:requested/:accepted dedup).
Tried to promote a 'rejected' proposal: NotApproved denial + durable ai_edit_promotion_denied receipt + PROMOTION_REJECTED event.
Tried model self-approval: blocked by runtime gate and DB regex.
Tried to create a half-promoted row (promotion ids set but state!='promoted', or state='promoted' with one id null): blocked by chk_..._promotion CHECK at the DB layer.
Tried approve diff A then verify B cannot masquerade: no binding exists -- the push path does not verify against diff_sha256. This is the integrity gap noted above (bounded to draft log).

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG negative test green (33 CRDT tests pass under runtime-full,test-utils).

evidence: Hardened d6adf2dd: AI-edit lease chokepoint + approved-vs-applied binding (migration 0192 CHECK applied_sha256=diff_sha256); applied_update_must_hash_to_approved_diff PASS.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
